title: Social Media and Mental Health
description: For questions 9 – 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Social media (0) has become an integral part of modern life, with billions of people using platforms like Facebook, Instagram, and Twitter daily. However, researchers are increasingly concerned about its impact on mental health, particularly among young people.||Studies have found a correlation (9) between heavy social media use and increased rates of anxiety and depression. One reason for this may/might/could (10) be that people constantly compare themselves to others' carefully curated online personas, (11) which can lead to feelings of inadequacy. The pressure to receive likes and comments (12) has created a culture where self-worth is often measured by online validation.||However, social media is not entirely negative. (13) It can help people stay connected with friends and family, especially those who live far away. Online communities provide support for individuals facing similar challenges, and social media (14) has proven effective for raising awareness about important social issues. Experts recommend limiting screen time and being mindful (15) of how social media makes you feel. (16) If you notice it affecting your mood negatively, it may be time to take a break and focus on real-world connections.

example.text: Social media (0) become an integral part of modern life.

questions.explanation: Expresión 'correlation between X and Y' indica relación entre dos factores.
Verbo modal con infinitivo: 'may/might/could be' expresa posibilidad.
Pronombre relativo que se refiere a toda la situación anterior.
Presente perfecto: 'has created' indica resultado de una acción.
Pronombre sujeto que se refiere a las redes sociales.
Presente perfecto: 'has proven' indica experiencia hasta el presente.
Expresión 'being mindful of' significa estar consciente de algo.
Conjunción condicional que introduce una posible situación.
